statements.sql: WITH org AS (SELECT 0 AS OrgLevel, NULL AS ManagerId, EmployeeID AS EmployeeId, CAST(LastName AS NVARCHAR(100)) AS FullPath FROM bronze.Employee WHERE ManagerID IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT o.OrgLevel + 1, e.ManagerID, e.EmployeeID, CAST(o.FullPath + ' > ' + e.LastName AS NVARCHAR(100)) FROM bronze.Employee AS e JOIN org AS o ON e.ManagerID = o.EmployeeId) INSERT INTO silver.RecursiveCteTarget (OrgLevel, ManagerId, EmployeeId, FullPath) SELECT OrgLevel, ManagerId, EmployeeId, FullPath FROM org

refactor.extracted_sql: WITH org AS ( SELECT 0 AS OrgLevel, NULL AS ManagerId, EmployeeID AS EmployeeId, CAST(LastName AS NVARCHAR(100)) AS FullPath FROM bronze.Employee WHERE ManagerID IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT o.OrgLevel + 1, e.ManagerID, e.EmployeeID, CAST(o.FullPath + ' > ' + e.LastName AS NVARCHAR(100)) FROM bronze.Employee e JOIN org o ON e.ManagerID = o.EmployeeId ) SELECT OrgLevel, ManagerId, EmployeeId, FullPath FROM org;
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT EmployeeID, ManagerID, LastName FROM bronze.Employee ), logical AS ( SELECT 0 AS OrgLevel, NULL AS ManagerId, EmployeeID AS EmployeeId, CAST(LastName AS NVARCHAR(100)) AS FullPath FROM import WHERE ManagerID IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT logical.OrgLevel + 1, import.ManagerID, import.EmployeeID, CAST(logical.FullPath + ' > ' + import.LastName AS NVARCHAR(100)) FROM import JOIN logical ON import.ManagerID = logical.EmployeeId ), final AS ( SELECT OrgLevel, ManagerId, EmployeeId, FullPath FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final;

source_tables.summary: Both queries source from bronze.Employee. Extracted uses direct reference; refactored uses an import CTE to extract the same table.

output_columns.summary: Both return OrgLevel (INT), ManagerId (INT), EmployeeId (INT), FullPath (NVARCHAR(100)) in the same order with identical types and casting.

joins.summary: Both perform identical recursive self-joins: the base case selects root employees (ManagerID IS NULL), then the recursive term joins on ManagerID = EmployeeId with path concatenation. Join logic is semantically equivalent.

filters.summary: Both apply WHERE ManagerID IS NULL in the base case of the CTE to identify root nodes. Filter logic is identical.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both are recursive CTEs producing one row per employee with organizational hierarchy level and manager chain. Granularity and output cardinality are equivalent.
